title: Cystic Fibrosis - New Diagnosis
chief_complaint: My baby has had a chronic cough since birth, frequent pneumonia (3 episodes), and he's not gaining weight. His stools are greasy, foul-smelling, and float. He tastes salty when I kiss him.

physical_exam.Growth: Weight <3rd percentile. Height 5th percentile. Failure to thrive.
physical_exam.Lungs: Bilateral crackles, rhonchi. Prolonged expiration. Barrel chest developing.

hidden_data.sweat_chloride: Sweat chloride 95 mmol/L (diagnostic ≥60). Repeat: 98 mmol/L. Confirms CF.
hidden_data.genetic: CFTR gene: F508del homozygous (most common severe mutation)
hidden_data.cxr: Hyperinflation. Peribronchial thickening. Bilateral upper lobe infiltrates. Mucus plugging.
hidden_data.fecal_elastase: Fecal elastase <15 mcg/g (severe pancreatic insufficiency - normal >200).
hidden_data.vitamins: Vitamin A, D, E, K deficient (fat-soluble vitamin deficiency from pancreatic insufficiency).

correct_diagnosis: Cystic Fibrosis (CFTR F508del Homozygous) with Pancreatic Insufficiency, Chronic Lung Disease, and Failure to Thrive
key_learning_points: CF = autosomal recessive disorder of CFTR gene on chromosome 7. Defective chloride channel → thick, viscous secretions in lungs, pancreas, sinuses, liver, intestines. Most common lethal genetic disease in Caucasians
Classic triad: Chronic sinopulmonary infections + Pancreatic insufficiency + Elevated sweat chloride. Newborn screening (IRT/DNA) identifies most cases
Sweat chloride test is diagnostic gold standard: ≥60 mmol/L = positive. 30-59 = intermediate (repeat). <30 = negative. Genetic testing confirms specific mutation

Respiratory: Airway clearance (chest PT, vest, PEP devices), Mucolytics (Dornase alfa - Pulmozyme, Hypertonic saline 7%), Bronchodilators, Antibiotics (chronic azithromycin, inhaled Tobramycin for Pseudomonas, IV for exacerbations). CFTR modulators (Ivacaftor, Lumacaftor/Ivacaftor, Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor - TRIKAFTA) are disease-modifying therapies based on genotype

Pancreatic: Pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (PERT - Creon) with every meal and snack. ADEK vitamins (fat-soluble). High-calorie, high-fat diet (120-150% normal). Gastrostomy tube if unable to meet caloric needs

Prognosis: Median survival now >50 years with early diagnosis and aggressive treatment. TRIKAFTA has revolutionized CF care for F508del patients. Monitor: PFTs q3 months, sputum cultures, annual labs, OGTT (CF-related diabetes), bone density
